meta.title: Ueno-no Ski Area
meta.description: Osaki, Miyagi — minutes from Naruko Onsen. Hot springs × snow play packages.

hero.title:
Ueno-no
Ski Area
hero.tagline: About 3 minutes from Naruko Onsen — a hidden hot-spring × snow-play park.

transit.title:
Shinkansen to
the snow.
transit.lead: About three hours from Tokyo. Exit Naruko-Onsen Station and a roughly 10-minute taxi ride—transit-resort travel with minimal friction.
transit.imgAlt: Travel image of snowy scenery seen from a Tohoku Shinkansen car

primary.title: About 3 minutes from the onsen town
primary.lead: Close enough for Naruko Onsen hotel guests to visit during the day. At ¥2,500 for a day ticket, even a short visit pays for itself.
primary.imgAlt: Travel image of snowy scenery seen from a Shinkansen car

secondary.title: Hot-spring hopping × snow play
secondary.lead: After skiing, head to the communal bath Taki-no-Yu—Naruko's iconic acidic spring with a thousand years of history.

onsen.title:
Naruko Onsen
is next door
onsen.lead: Naruko Onsen brings together 17 springs and varied water types. Hop between Waseda-sajiki-yu and Naruko Hotel's famous "eel hot spring."
onsen.whisper: Bundled "onsen tour × snow play" packages with local inns are a fast path to higher revenue.
onsen.imgAlt: Image of an outdoor bath surrounded by forest

powder.title: Snow-play park for the onsen town
powder.body: Lead with sledding and tubing zones. Position as a Snow Play Area for Naruko hotel guests who want snow without expert ski skills.

shinkansen.title: Go by Shinkansen
shinkansen.body: Tokyo ~3 hours → Naruko-Onsen Station → 10-minute taxi. Design a day of extremes: Tokyo morning, slopes afternoon, onsen evening.

beginners.title: Beginners & families
beginners.body: Gentle pitches suited to families with small children. Pair first-time snow play with Naruko inn packages for international visitors.

access.note: About 10 minutes by taxi from Tohoku Shinkansen Naruko-Onsen Station (approx.)
